dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal captures failed breakouts/breakdowns using Donchian channels to define range boundaries (dc_upper/dc_lower). Entry conditions detect price breaking past boundary then reversing: LONG when price breaks above upper boundary then pulls back but still above prior close (trapped shorts covering); SHORT when price breaks below lower boundary then rebounds but still below prior close (trapped longs covering). Exit conditions are simple reversals of the reversal move. Magnitude_of_move and specific boundary detection method left to backtest engine discretion per AUTHOR_SILENT parameters.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal captures failed breakouts/breakdowns using Donchian channels to define range boundaries (dc_upper/dc_lower). Entry conditions detect price breaking past boundary then reversing: LONG when price breaks above upper boundary then pulls back but still above prior close (trapped shorts covering); SHORT when price breaks below lower boundary then rebounds but still below prior close (trapped longs covering). Exit conditions are simple reversals of the reversal move. Magnitude_of_move and specific boundary detection method left to backtest engine discretion per AUTHOR_SILENT parameters.

source_rule_text: Trade the failed range breakouts and breakdowns where price reverses sharply after breaking a boundary, as trapped traders covering positions create sharp moves.
